task_completion.description: Navigate the map view to Denali National Park or Mount Denali.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main map view displays the terrain of Mount Denali or Denali National Park in Alaska.

task_completion.description: Open the dialog to create a new placemark.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'New Placemark' or similar dialog box is open on the screen.
The 'Name' text field in the placemark dialog contains exactly 'Denali Summit'.

task_completion.description: Position the placemark precisely at the peak of Mount Denali.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The placemark icon (e.g., a pushpin or target) is placed exactly on the highest visual point (summit) of the mountain in the map view.

task_completion.description: Include the elevation in the placemark description.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Description' text area in the placemark dialog contains an elevation value (e.g., numbers indicating feet or meters).
The placemark named 'Denali Summit' is visible in the 'My Places' section of the sidebar or places panel.

integrity.description: The agent used a genuine mapping/GIS application (like Google Earth Pro) rather than a text editor or web search.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the UI of a mapping application with 3D/2D terrain, not a web browser showing a search engine/Wikipedia or a text editor.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the application's GUI to create the placemark.
integrity.visual_evidence: Dialog boxes, toolbars, and sidebar panels are actively used, indicating manual GUI interaction rather than terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass the UI by writing a KML script in a terminal or editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Absence of terminal windows, code editors, or command-line interfaces being used to generate the placemark file directly.
